storyboard.content: I've been having a burning pain in my upper stomach — kind of in the middle, below my chest. It's been bothering me for a few months now.
I feel bloated a lot more than I used to, and sometimes food just feels like it's sitting in my stomach and not moving. It's an uncomfortable heavy feeling.
The symptoms started gradually about 3 months ago and have become more consistent and predictable over the last 6 weeks.
The burning is worst between meals and at night, but eating something makes it feel better for a little while. After 2 to 3 hours though, the pain comes right back.
Over-the-counter antacids give me some relief but it doesn't last — the symptoms keep coming back no matter what I try.
The discomfort is moderate — I can still work and do most things normally, but I've changed what and how much I eat because I'm worried about triggering the pain.
I've been eating less than usual because I worry the pain will come back when I eat.
I'm a 42-year-old male. I haven't lost any weight.
